raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I attempted to use my Citi XXXX Credit Card to make a {$4500.00} down payment for new cabinet installation. The transaction failed at the time and was canceled by the merchant. 
Several weeks later, I discovered the {$4500.00} charge had posted to my account. I contacted the merchant, who confirmed in writing via text that this was a duplicate payment and provided a copy of his bank statement showing the funds were returned to Citibank. 

I filed a Duplicate Payment / Pay by Other Means dispute with Citibank. They requested more information but allowed only two documents to be uploaded, even though I had multiple pieces of relevant evidence. I expected further communication to submit the rest, but instead I received an email stating the case was closed and my provisional credit was reversed.

I later resubmitted all evidence, including : The contract showing the work was paid by other means Text messages from the merchant confirming the duplicate payment The merchants bank statement showing reversal of funds to Citibank Citibank reopened the case but did not restore provisional credit. They also reported to credit bureaus that I was over my limit. 
From late XXXX through XX/XX/year>, I called Citibank almost daily for updates. Every time, I was told the case was still processing and that there was no way to contact the dispute department. Supervisors confirmed this information. On XX/XX/XXXX, I was transferred to the XXXX Department, where I was told the case had actually been closed since XXXX XXXX everything I had been told. The Executive Department apologized and stated the case would be reopened.

I believe Citibank closed the case without fully reviewing the evidence and may have intentionally delayed providing accurate updates to run out the legal 90-day dispute resolution period.
